# datadog_downtime (Data Source)

Use this data source to retrieve information about an existing Datadog downtime.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "datadog_downtime" "example" {
  id = "11111111-2222-3333-4444-555555555555"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `id` (String) The ID of this downtime data source.

### Read-Only

- `canceled` (String) Time that the downtime was canceled.
- `created` (String) Creation time of the downtime.
- `display_timezone` (String) The timezone in which to display the downtime's start and end times in Datadog applications. This is not used
as an offset for scheduling.
- `message` (String) A message to include with notifications for this downtime. Email notifications can be sent to specific users
by using the same `@username` notation as events.
- `modified` (String) Time that the downtime was last modified.
- `mute_first_recovery_notification` (Boolean) If the first recovery notification during a downtime should be muted.
- `notify_end_states` (List of String) States that will trigger a monitor notification when the `notify_end_types` action occurs.
- `notify_end_types` (List of String) Actions that will trigger a monitor notification if the downtime is in the `notify_end_types` state.
- `scope` (String) The scope to which the downtime applies. Must follow the [common search syntax](https://docs.datadoghq.com/logs/explorer/search_syntax/).
- `status` (String) The current status of the downtime.
